mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward as investors price in stronger corporate earnings from increased consumer spending over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 labor market recovery post-financial crisis
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains elevated above 4.5% while inflation-adjusted real rates stay positive, constraining equity valuations. A sustained period of restrictive monetary policy depresses forward equity returns as the cost of capital rises relative to expected cash flows.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI inflation) > 1.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with positive real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite restrictive real rate signal, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates or contracts month-over-month, indicating weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term earnings visibility. Equity investors reprrice risk downward, producing negative 5-day forward returns as sentiment shifts toward defensive positioning.
failure_condition: Weak retail sales trigger does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above its 3-month moving average, signaling expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength. This supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days as firms operate at higher efficiency.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating a dual demand shock in residential construction and labor markets. This joint deterioration signals recession risk and equity investors reduce exposure, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in prior month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 housing at 1354k units, unemployment at 4.1%
failure_condition: Joint housing-unemployment deterioration signal does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines below 4.0%, signaling tight labor markets and wage pressure. Equity investors interpret this as inflationary risk that will force the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 unemployment at 4.1%, near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Low unemployment trigger does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously expand, indicating broad-based demand strength. This dual confirmation of economic expansion drives equity valuations upward as earnings growth expectations rise over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 GDP at 23386.2B, retail sales at 718867M
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Dual GDP-retail sales expansion does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains above 4.5% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) decelerates, creating a regime of restrictive real monetary policy. Equity investors anticipate eventual rate cuts but face near-term headwinds from elevated cost of capital, producing negative 5-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2010 Fed funds at 4.83%, CPI at 315.45
failure_condition: Restrictive real rate signal does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 18-month windows.
